name: Royal Leamington Spa, England
significance: Birthplace and the opening scene of the family pressure that Crowley later turned into anti-religious self-making.

name: Boleskine House, Loch Ness
significance: The Abramelin site that became one of the signature places in Crowley's magical geography.

name: London, England
significance: The crowded center of Golden Dawn politics, publishing, and much of Crowley's public life.

name: Cairo, Egypt
significance: The Cairo Working city where Liber AL and the Thelemic narrative of revelation take shape.

name: Bou Saada, Algeria
significance: Desert base for the Enochian Aethyr work with Victor Neuburg and the abyssal visionary current.

name: Paris, France
significance: A recurring writing city for exile, publishing, and the later French phase of Crowley's career.

name: Cefalu, Sicily
significance: Home of the Abbey of Thelema, the most visible attempt to make his system into a lived community.

name: New York City, USA
significance: A key American city for lectures, exile, occult networking, and the postwar afterlife of his work.

name: Hastings, England
significance: Crowley's final home, where the public legend and the private body meet at the end of the archive.

name: Blythe Road, London
significance: Administrative ground zero for Golden Dawn conflict and one of the order's most contested addresses.

name: Redhill, Surrey, England
significance: The family base after Leamington, where religious discipline and domestic surveillance sharpened.

name: Cambridge, England
significance: Trinity College and the undergraduate setting where Crowley's literary and occult self-formation accelerates.

name: Schonbuhl Glacier, Swiss Alps
significance: Alpine terrain that fed Crowley's self-image as climber, ordeal-taker, and a man of will.

name: Karakoram / K2
significance: The expedition zone where Crowley tested stamina, fellowship, and the mythology of endurance.

name: Darjeeling, India
significance: A Himalayan staging point for travel, study, and the eastern routes in Crowley's biography.

name: Tunis, Tunisia
significance: The North African exile stop after the Abbey years collapsed and the community unraveled.

name: Berlin, Germany
significance: A Weimar-era hub for art, sex, and social experimentation in Crowley's wider orbit.

name: Oxford, England
significance: A site of student life, literary contact, and the social worlds surrounding Crowley's circle.

name: Caxton Hall, London
significance: The public performance venue where Crowley tested ritual theater before an audience.

name: Pasadena, California
significance: A later American occult afterlife site linked to Jack Parsons and the Agape Lodge.
